I just keep filling out the spreadsheets the way I was told to, even though the process feels broken.
I am waiting for my manager to tell me what my priorities should be for the coming week.

I hope the leadership team decides to give us more headcount so that my current workload becomes manageable.
I followed the original project plan exactly as written because I didn't want to be responsible for any deviations.
I feel completely overwhelmed by the crowd and am just waiting for someone to tell me which patient I should look at first.
I hope the surgical team knows the patient is coming, because I don't know how to contact them or what the protocol is.
I am just following the queue as it happens and hoping that no one's condition worsens while they wait for me.

I let the paint drift across the canvas, accepting wherever the colors decide to settle.
I simply use whatever tools happen to be within reach and hope for a fortunate accident.
I wait for a sudden spark of inspiration to strike before I feel capable of picking up a brush.
I leave the prose as it first came to me, fearing that any change would disrupt the natural flow.
I am content to let the audience find their own meaning in the chaos I've left behind.
I am simply waiting for the opposing counsel to send over their document production so I can see what we are dealing with.
I hope the judge finds the current filings sufficient and decides to move the case forward without requiring me to file additional motions.

I am open to whatever settlement figure the other side proposes, provided it resolves the matter without further stress.
I am simply shifted by the wind and dissolved by the rain, settling wherever the topography dictates my final resting place.
I absorb whatever minerals happen to drift into my root zone, existing only as a passive recipient of the environment's whims.
I wait in the dormant seed bank for decades, awakening only when external conditions happen to trigger my germination.
I am carved and reshaped by the current, my banks eroding slowly as the water dictates the curve of my existence.
I drift through the water column as plankton, consumed by whoever happens to swim through my current coordinates.
I prefer a centralized orchestrator that explicitly dictates every state transition to ensure the system remains entirely predictable and controllable.
I rely on a strict, synchronous request-response hierarchy to ensure that no action occurs without a direct command from a superior layer.
I define static routing tables and hard-coded configuration files to prevent any unexpected deviations in how requests are handled.
I set fixed thread pools and static memory limits to maintain a rigid environment where resource usage is capped and unchanging.
I require a manual sign-off and a predefined checklist for every deployment to ensure a human operator validates every single change.
I am happy to go wherever you think is best, as I trust your judgment completely.
I usually just wait to see how you feel about our spending before I form an opinion on our budget.
I was hoping you would suggest something to do, but I'm fine with whatever you decide.
I tend to just let things happen naturally and hope that everything works out in the end.
I often find myself adapting my own needs to fit your preferences just to keep the peace.
I just wait for the city council to tell me where the new zoning lines will be drawn.
I hope someone eventually notices that the bike lanes in the east are missing.
I follow the existing traffic patterns because that is how the roads have always been laid out.
I attend the community workshops to see what the neighbors decide they want.
I use the lighting system that was installed ten years ago because it still works.
I simply sit in the silence and let the stillness carry me wherever it needs to go.
I surrender my pain to the universe, trusting that it will dissolve on its own time.
I drift with the current of grace, allowing my spiritual evolution to unfold without my interference.
I wait for the truth to reveal itself to me through a sudden, unbidden moment of clarity.
I open myself as a vessel, hoping to be filled by a will far greater than my own.
